Brittany uses 1/4 ounce of shampoo every other day, which means she uses [number_of_uses] ounces per week. In a leap year, there are 366 days, so she will wash her hair [number_of_times] times. To calculate the total amount of shampoo needed, multiply the number of uses by 1/4 ounce. This will give the total ounces required for the entire year. Once this total is determined, divide it by 14 to find out how many bottles she needs to buy.

Since each bottle contains 14 ounces, Brittany will need to purchase [number_of_bottles] bottles to last through the leap year. It’s important to round up to the nearest whole number if there is any remainder, as partial bottles cannot be purchased. This ensures she has enough shampoo for the entire year without running out. The exact number of bottles depends on the total usage, which is based on [number_of_times] washes in a leap year.
